query: Provide a comprehensive landscape of commercial powder dispensing solutions on the market today. List companies and their flagship products that offer automated or semi-automated powder dispensing/dosing systems for laboratory or industrial use. For each, summarize the dispensing technology (e.g., gravimetric, volumetric, vibratory, screw/auger, acoustic, capacitive), supported powder types, dose range (mg to kg), accuracy, throughput, automation/integration features (robotics, API, LIMS), and approximate price tier. Include both bench-top lab dispensers and larger industrial systems.

List companies producing automated laboratory powder dispensing instruments suitable for chemistry/materials research labs, with model names. Include at minimum: Mettler Toledo Quantos, Chemspeed (e.g., SWING, FLEX, Crystal), Unchained Labs / Freeslate, Symyx, Zinsser Analytic Lissy, Gilson, Hamilton, Sartorius, Analytik Jena, Anton Paar, Retsch / Verder, Schenck Process, K-Tron / Coperion, Brabender Technologie, Gericke, Plasmatreat, AutoDose, Powder Systems Limited (PSL), Glatt, GEA. For each company describe relevant powder-handling products and key specs.

Compare the underlying technologies used in commercial powder dispensing equipment: gravimetric loss-in-weight feeders, volumetric screw/auger feeders, vibratory feeders, acoustic dispensers, micro-dosing heads (Mettler Quantos style), tapping-dispense modules, and robotic spatula/scoop systems. Discuss accuracy/precision, minimum dose, suitability for cohesive vs free-flowing powders, contamination/cross-contamination risk, throughput, and integration with robotic platforms. Cite vendor specifications and peer-reviewed comparisons where available.

Survey industrial bulk powder dispensing and feeding equipment used in pharma, food, plastics, additive manufacturing, and chemical process industries. Cover Coperion K-Tron, Schenck Process, Brabender Technologie, Gericke, Acrison, Hapman, AZO, GEA, Glatt, Powder Systems Limited (PSL), Matcon, MTI Mixers, Hosokawa Micron. Describe loss-in-weight feeders, IBC/bin dispensing, split-butterfly valves, and contained transfer systems. Provide model names, capacities, and notable end-users/applications.

What commercial instruments can dispense powders at the milligram or sub-milligram scale with high precision? Focus on Mettler Toledo Quantos QX1/QX2, Chemspeed Powdernium / GDU, Freeslate / Unchained Labs Core Module Powder Dispensing, Zinsser Lissy, Analytik Jena CyBio, Hamilton STAR with powder head, and any acoustic or piezo-based micro-powder dispensers. Provide minimum dose, accuracy (±%), powder compatibility, and references to publications using these systems for high-throughput experimentation.

Provide a detailed catalog of MTI Corporation (mtixtl.com) products related to powder handling, dispensing, dosing, weighing, mixing, and transfer for laboratory and pilot-scale materials research (especially battery, ceramics, and additive-manufacturing R&D). For each relevant MTI product line (e.g., automatic powder weighing/dispensing stations, glovebox-compatible powder feeders, vibratory sieve shakers, rotary tablet/pellet presses, planetary ball mills with powder loading, slurry mixers, tape casters with powder hoppers, and any robotic/automation accessories) list the model number, dispensing or feeding mechanism, dose / capacity range, accuracy or repeatability, supported powder types (free-flowing vs cohesive, particle size), automation/control interfaces (RS-232, USB, Modbus, Ethernet, API), glovebox / inert-atmosphere compatibility, and approximate price tier where stated. Cite MTI datasheets, manuals, and any peer-reviewed papers using MTI powder equipment.

resubmissions.reason: Original task stuck at 99% (get_task returns HTTP 500). Per PR feedback, re-run just one query and don't wait.

query: Survey MTI Mixers (mtimixer.com) and MTI Group industrial powder handling equipment used in food, chemical, pharmaceutical, and plastics processing. Include batch and continuous mixers, dispensing/dosing stations, IBC / bin handling, vacuum conveying, and weighing systems. For each named model or product line, give capacity (kg or L per batch, kg/h continuous), feeding/dispensing principle, accuracy where reported, integration with PLC/SCADA, containment level, and notable end-user industries or installations. Distinguish MTI Mixers from the unrelated MTI Corporation (materials lab equipment) and from Mischtechnik International (MTI) GmbH if applicable.

Provide model-level technical specifications for industrial powder handling and dispensing equipment from the following vendors, where evidence from datasheets or peer-reviewed/trade literature is available: (1) Acrison weight-loss differential and volumetric feeders (e.g., Models 105, 130, 140, 170, 270, 402, 403, 405, BDF, MBDF); (2) Hapman bulk-bag unloaders, tubular drag conveyors, helix flexible screw conveyors, PosiPortion feeders; (3) AZO (AZO GmbH + Co. KG) ingredient automation, DOSITAINER, SHIFTSIFTER, ROTAJET, FLEXSCREEN, COMPONENTER manual/automatic small- and micro-component weighing systems; (4) Powder Systems Limited (PSL) GFD, GFD Lab, PSL Vac-Tech containment systems and powder handling isolators; (5) Matcon IBC systems with cone valve technology, IBC blenders, and automated discharge stations. For each, capture throughput, accuracy/precision, dose or batch range, containment level (OEB), and integration features. Note any items where vendor-level evidence is not available rather than guessing.

Investigate which mainstream liquid-handling and lab-automation platforms offer optional powder / solid-dispensing modules or accessories, and what their actual specifications are. Cover at minimum: (1) Hamilton Microlab STAR / STARlet / Vantage with any solid-dispensing or 'powder head' option, and Hamilton's relationship to Mettler Toledo Quantos integrations on the deck; (2) Tecan Fluent / Freedom EVO with solid-dispensing accessories; (3) Zinsser Analytic Lissy and SpeedyDoser solid dispensers; (4) Gilson PIPETMAX / GX-271 with any solid-dispensing add-ons; (5) Sartorius arium / Cubis II MCA balances integrated with robotic solid dispensing; (6) Analytik Jena CyBio FeliX / Well with powder accessories; (7) Anton Paar accessories for solids in rheology / density platforms. For each give product/module name, dispensing principle, dose range, accuracy, and how it is exposed to scripting / API control.

Locate datasheet- or paper-grade evidence for: (a) acoustic, piezo, or capacitive *dry-powder* microdispensers, including EDC Biosystems ATS Gen5/Gen6 acoustic dispensers used for solid particles, Scienion sciDROP-PICO solid handling options, Labcyte/Beckman Echo dry-powder evaluations, and any academic prototypes for nanoparticle / microparticle acoustic ejection; (b) the specific Mettler Toledo Quantos QX1 and QX2 modules — minimum dose, accuracy, throughput, head types, balance compatibility (XPR/XPE), and integration options; (c) Chemspeed 'Powdernium' branding and the GDU family (GDU-V, GDU-Pfd, GDU-S/SWILE, GDU-P) — minimum dose, accuracy at sub-mg / mg / 10 mg / 100 mg targets, container volumes, supported powder cohesiveness, and dispense-time benchmarks. Be explicit where information is not retrievable.

Survey academic and open-source robotic powder dispensing systems published in the last ~10 years, especially those used in self-driving labs and high-throughput experimentation for chemistry, materials, and battery research. Include: dual-arm biomimetic dispensing (Cooper group, Liverpool, 2023); the Berkeley A-Lab and related autonomous labs; Coscientist / ChemOS / ARES style platforms with solid handling; FLIP-style flow-aware dosing using ML; Opentrons-based powder dispensing add-ons; any LEGO/Arduino/3D-printed open-hardware dispensers; integration of 6-axis cobots (UR3/UR5/Franka) with balances. For each, report mechanism (spatula, scoop, vibratory, screw, electrostatic), demonstrated dose range and accuracy, powder types tested, throughput, and code/CAD availability. Also identify what *hardware/software gaps* these works highlight relative to commercial Quantos/Chemspeed systems.
